tactical_shape: Obdurate, functional side that is often defensive through a lack of attacking talent
Should not attack with gay abandon against Brazil; likely to be safety-first if pressed
Need to show a little more invention rather than being one-dimensional
standout_players: Andy Robertson: captain and key voice, said he couldn't care less about third-place permutations before the game
Ben Gannon-Doak: described as 'almost an old-school winger'
fatigue_injuries: Aaron Hickey injured and unavailable; hoped to be ready for the knockout stages
home_read.momentum: In control of their own destiny and on the brink of historic knockout qualification, but under pressure to avoid being pummelled by Brazil and to show more invention.

tactical_shape: A hugely dangerous, quality-packed team with a strong attacking identity; 'the characteristics of a Brazil team is that they have to attack'
Expected to threaten in every position, with Neymar potentially starting or coming off the bench to lift them
Regarded as one of the best teams in the world and a likely last-four side
standout_players: Neymar: returning from injury, described by Steve Clarke as an 'icon', 'superstar of the modern era' and a dangerous opponent even off the bench
Vinícius Júnior, Endrick, Martinelli: named by Robertson as part of Brazil's many quality options
away_read.momentum: Viewed as a formidable, elite opponent with serious tournament ambitions; even from the bench Neymar would give them a lift.
